title: Module 0 Project: Student Enrollment Activity Roster
summary: Build a qualified and readable student-enrollment roster through five cumulative join steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Project Overview: Building a Student Enrollment Roster
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a comprehensive student enrollment roster by joining data from the `students` and `enrollments` tables. The goal is to produce a readable and qualified report that lists each student's enrollment activities. You'll use SQL joins to connect these tables, ensuring that each enrollment record is accurately linked to the corresponding student. This project will guide you through five steps, each building on the previous one, to achieve a final, polished roster.

quizDraft.title: Connect Students to Enrollment Records
quizDraft.prompt: Use an INNER JOIN to connect students and enrollments. Return student id, student name, enrollment id, course id, and term.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT 
  students.id, 
  students.name, 
  enrollments.id, 
  enrollments.course_id, 
  enrollments.term 
FROM students 
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  students.id, 
  students.name, 
  enrollments.id, 
  enrollments.course_id, 
  enrollments.term 
FROM students 
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
quizDraft.hint: Join the students and enrollments tables using the student_id foreign key.

help.concept: INNER JOIN connects rows from two tables based on a related column.
help.hint_1: Use INNER JOIN to combine students and enrollments on student_id.
help.hint_2: Select the necessary columns from both tables after joining.

quizDraft.title: Qualify Every Shared Identifier
quizDraft.prompt: Keep the same rows and qualify every selected id column with its table name or alias.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT 
  students.id AS student_id, 
  students.name, 
  enrollments.id AS enrollment_id, 
  enrollments.course_id, 
  enrollments.term 
FROM students 
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  students.id AS student_id, 
  students.name, 
  enrollments.id AS enrollment_id, 
  enrollments.course_id, 
  enrollments.term 
FROM students 
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
quizDraft.hint: Use table names or aliases to qualify each id column.

help.concept: Qualifying columns helps avoid ambiguity when multiple tables have columns with the same name.
help.hint_1: Add table names before each id column in the SELECT statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure each id column is prefixed with its table name or alias.

quizDraft.title: Add Readable Table and Column Aliases
quizDraft.prompt: Use concise table aliases and rename the displayed identifiers as student_id, enrollment_id, and course_id.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id;
quizDraft.hint: Introduce table aliases and rename columns for clarity.

help.concept: Table and column aliases make SQL queries more readable and concise.
help.hint_1: Use AS to rename columns and introduce table aliases.
help.hint_2: Ensure each column has a clear and descriptive alias.

quizDraft.title: Focus on Upper Grade Levels
quizDraft.prompt: Keep the complete joined roster and include only students in grade level 10 or higher.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id 
WHERE s.grade_level >= 10;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id 
WHERE s.grade_level >= 10;
quizDraft.hint: Filter the results to include only students in grade 10 or above.

help.concept: The WHERE clause filters rows based on specified conditions.
help.hint_1: Use a WHERE clause to filter students by grade level.
help.hint_2: Ensure the condition checks for grade levels 10 or higher.

quizDraft.title: Order the Roster Predictably
quizDraft.prompt: Sort by student name ascending, then course_id ascending, while preserving the complete query.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id 
WHERE s.grade_level >= 10 
ORDER BY s.name ASC, e.course_id ASC;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id 
WHERE s.grade_level >= 10 
ORDER BY s.name ASC, e.course_id ASC;
quizDraft.hint: Use ORDER BY to sort the results by student name and course_id.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts the result set based on specified columns.
help.hint_1: Add an ORDER BY clause to sort by student name and course_id.
help.hint_2: Ensure both columns are sorted in ascending order.
